question.text: What is the maximum percentage of IPO proceeds that can be allocated towards General Corporate Purposes (GCP)?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under Regulation 7 of SEBI ICDR, the amount earmarked for General Corporate Purposes (GCP) cannot exceed 25% of the total amount raised. Additionally, GCP plus unidentified acquisitions together cannot exceed 35% of the total. **The Trap:** 35% is the combined cap for GCP plus unidentified acquisitions — not the GCP-only cap. Candidates who remember only the higher number may select this incorrectly. **Cross-Exam Context:** This concept of end-use restrictions appears in NISM Series V-A (Mutual Fund) as well, where fund managers must deploy capital per stated objectives. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** When drafting the objects of the issue, ensure clear delineation between GCP and specific project costs. SEBI scrutinizes vague fund utilization plans, and excessive GCP allocation may invite adverse observations.
